text_content: This analysis investigates all the aspect of the device from a bottom-up perspective. It identifies any inconsistency, design flaws and potential implementation failures. This FMEA concentrates on the investigation of: a) the architecture solution, its stability, adequateness, and flexibility b) the design and any components, its complexity, reliability, performance, effectiveness, criticality, and its implementation c) any interfaces within the components and 3rd-party applications d) any SOUP components e) Packaging, transportation and storage During the Analysis phase, if it is determined that a failure of a component / sub-system could also result in risk, an additional scenario is added to the RMR. This may result in additional hazard identification where top-down analysis did not identify potential hazards, but in most cases isolates additional contributory factors for existing hazards.

title: 8.0 Production and Post-production Information
text_content: Production and post-production information will be continually monitored and analyzed to determine if new risks have been identified and to review the assessments of known risks to maintain an effective risk management process. This information includes, but is not limited to, design and process changes, revisions of standards, identifications of defects, and reports of complaints. In addition to Varian’s information about the device, the following shall be reviewed: • Information on similar devices on the market; • Publicly available information such as recalls, FDA Medical Device Reports, and vigilance reports; • Scientific literature. Analysis of relevant data is used to demonstrate that risk-based decisions and risk control measures for this device are appropriate and effective. This Risk Management Plan documents the specific activities and processes undertaken to ensure the ongoing safety of this device throughout the product life-cycle. Potential effects of device hazards may impact manufacturing personnel, patients, operators, service personnel, bystanders and the environment in which the device is used (including interactions with other 3rd-party equipment).  All of these elements shall be evaluated as part of the risk assessment. Project risks such as schedule, budget, and resources are outside the scope of this RMP.

title: 3. DAEN > 7.1.8 Conclusions of the Analysis of Manufacturer Data (Continued)
text_content: Mitigations through product design and labeling are in place. The mitigations were derived from thorough risk analysis processes through which risk control measures are formulated, implemented, and validated during product development. The risk control measures have been tested through verification and validation activities, and through product usability studies. The risks and their associated potential hazards have been assessed via analysis of available clinical data regarding the use of HAL. The safety findings in the clinical data were analyzed in context of the benefits to the patients as described above. Clinical data was analyzed for any evidence of the residual risks of HAL 4.0 identified in the RMR report. No adverse events or clinical toxicities were reported as attributable to the residual risks outlined in Section 5.1.4 (e.g. radiation over/under exposure, collision).

title: 7.0 Verification Activities > 7.1 Risk control measures
text_content: The three risk control options shall be considered in their order of priority: o Inherently safe design and manufacture; o Protective measures in the device itself or in the manufacturing process; o Information for safety (warnings/precautions/contra-indications) and, where appropriate, training to users. All recognized risk control measures shall be implemented to reduce risk to or maintain risk at an acceptable level. Implementation of risk control measures shall continue until successive risk control options or measures do not further reduce the estimated risk. Whenever possible, requirements of international standards or their corresponding harmonized versions shall be utilized as risk control measures. These applied standards are listed or referenced in Appendix B of this RMP. The implementation of risk control measures shall be verified. The effectiveness of the implemented risk control measures shall be verified or, as appropriate, validated.

text_content: Varian utilizes a process based on the Rational Unified Process, and is defined in document reference DND01 Design and Development. This procedure defines the phases of a project, and more specifically, the various documentation requirements for each project phase to provide a high level of confidence in both the product, and the processes used to develop that product. The procedure further defines the level of management oversight necessary, in the form of Phase Gates at the end of each prescribed phase. The main purpose of the procedure is to comply with global regulatory requirements for design control, in particular US FDA 21 CFR 820, and ISO 13485. A second document, RSK01 Medical Device Risk Management is used to supplement DND01 and provides guidance on Risk Management consistent with EN ISO 14971:2019 Error! Reference source not found.. text_content: D56979 HAL 4.0 Risk Management Report identifies safety-related use errors, their design mitigators, and the risk control operator for the design mitigators. Design mitigators where a human is the risk control operator are considered for inclusion in the summative usability testing.  Design mitigators that identify the risk control operators as the device are not included in the summative testing. Hazard-related use scenarios to be included in the summative test were identified from these reports.  The product team selected a subset of the human factor related hazard-related use scenarios based on the severity of the potential harm that could be caused by use error (e.g., for which medical intervention would be needed). During the risk hazard analysis meetings, the team identified which risks have a human factor (HF) cause type. title: 6. Best Practices to Mitigate Risks and Impacts on UA Completion (Continued)
text_content: Use contractor, intern, or other resources for administrative work and peer reviews. Late or no review responses impacts completeness or accuracy of UA. Proactively alert reviewers to schedule and procure reviewers’ commitment. Work with team to designate review proxies or assign backup reviewers. For no responses, assume acceptance. Delay in feature development, code implementation, or code freeze impacts completeness of UA. Reschedule work or UA schedule. Reprioritize work. Escalate to management to assign additional development resources or reset priorities. Work with product management to simulate workflow and use cases. Defer feature content until next release. Late UI changes impact accuracy of UA. Develop basic (“minimal viable”) content for safe and effective use. Eliminate or reduce graphic contents. CMS production issues--technological or knowledge- based—slow or prevent work and progress. Plan work incrementally to allow local work that is synched to network or system.

source: D56979 HAL 4.0 Risk Management Report rev07.pdf
title: 4.0 Review of Risk Management Activities > 4.1 Implementation of the risk management plan
text_content: A comprehensive risk study has been conducted for HAL 4.0 in accordance with ISO 14971:2019 (1) and Varian’s Risk Management Process. The study was conducted by cross-functional teams consisting of the members identified in Table 1. The teams reviewed risk to health posed by all persons interacting with HAL 4.0. This analysis considered hazards that could occur both in normal use (or foreseeable misuse) and hazards associated with potential system or sub-system failures. Hazards, hazardous situations, and harms have been identified using SHaF (System Hazard Framework). The hazards, hazardous situations, and harms, applicable to HAL 4.0 are documented in HAL 4.0 Elaboration Phase RHA (10). The risks associated with these hazards and hazardous situations have been assessed and risk control measures have been identified. The risks associated with these hazards and hazardous situations as well as the risk control measures are documented in this report in Appendix B: Risk Assessment.

text_content: Medical Systems. It may not be copied or reproduced without prior written permission from Varian Medical Systems. Doc Number Rev. Date Date of last e- D78266 01 Sign 7. Maintenance Plan Varian implements a product lifecycle framework that guides the incorporation of the Secure by Design principle in Varian products and solutions. The lifecycle framework also addresses the continuous management of cybersecurity risks after the products or solutions are released to the field. As part of the post-market cybersecurity maintenance, cybersecurity risk assessments are conducted periodically, or whenever it is needed in response to post-market cybersecurity signals such as 3rd party component vulnerability disclosure (e.g., critical Microsoft vulnerability CVE-2019-0708). Depending on nature and the level of risks, the results of the post-market cybersecurity risk assessments can lead to product updates, bug fixes, field services, Product Advisory, or Product Security Notification.

Elimination/reduction AFAP; risks must be reduced as far 5 as possible and not just to an “acceptable” level; no trade-offs via risk acceptance alone. 8.1 (option analysis), 8.2–8.5, 9 (overall residual risk), 3.4 (benefit–risk) Process demands progressive reduction and benefit–risk justification for residual risks; +A11 clarifies MDR’s AFAP expectation via Annex ZA notes.
